stem: A cube has a surface area of 96 square meters. What is the volume, in cubic meters, of the cube?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. A cube with side length s has surface area 6s². Setting 6s² = 96 gives s² = 16, so s = 4. The volume is s³ = 64 cubic meters.

Choice A gives s². Choice B doubles s². Choice D is 4&sup4;.
